title: ODBC Data Packing
text: ODBC Data Packing The Uniface ODBC configurator determines what storage format (data type) the DBMS supports. For any format the DBMS does not support, the connector uses the default storage format. For more information about the storage formats used by ODBC, see your DBMS documentation and your ODBC documentation. In Uniface documentation, the term storage format is therefore used to refer to DBMS data types . A Uniface data type is specified for each field in a modeled entity; a DBMS storage format is internal to the DBMS. Packing Codes Packing codes are mappings between Uniface data types and DBMS data types (storage formats). When writing a field, Uniface passes the data and a valid packing code to the DBMS connector, which uses the packing code to determine the appropriate storage format for storing the data in the target DBMS. Packing codes also determine how data is interfaced with 3GL routines. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
